meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Stop staring at a blank screen. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build and ship professional apps in hours, not weeks.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is a failure of process, not creativity. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you shift from ideation to execution. Use Antigravity to scaffold production-grade architecture and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, allowing you to ship functional, revenue-generating SaaS products rapidly.
content.intro:
The screen is glaring white, the cursor is blinking, and your mental energy is evaporating. You have the technical skills to build, but you're trapped in a paralysis of 'what to build.' This is the freelancer’s silent killer: the inability to bridge the gap between a blank repository and a deployable MVP.

But the most elite builders don't start from scratch; they start from a system. By adopting a high-velocity SaaS starter kit, you move from zero to production by simply filling in the blanks. We are going to dismantle the friction of starting, using the industrial-grade power of Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your next weekend project into a client-ready asset.

steps.title: Define the Architecture
steps.description: Establish your tech stack constraints to prevent feature creep before you write a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Architect. Define a minimalist, high-performance SaaS stack using Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind. Create a directory structure that prioritizes modularity for future client scaling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure based on the Antigravity architecture schema provided.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Authentication Layer
steps.description: Don't reinvent the wheel. Secure your app with pre-built auth flows that are ready for production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure, role-based authentication boilerplate using Supabase Auth. Include middleware for protected routes and automatic user session handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the auth middleware and login page templates as defined in the scaffold.'

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Use automated schema generation to ensure your database is strictly typed and synced with your frontend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a SQL schema for a SaaS multi-tenant application. Include tables for profiles, subscriptions, and usage logs. Provide the migration script.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Apply the generated SQL schema to the local Supabase instance.'

steps.title: Implement High-Conversion UI Components
steps.description: Build the core user experience using pre-optimized, accessible components that look professional out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a landing page component library featuring a hero section, pricing table, and feature grid using Shadcn/UI and Tailwind CSS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Scaffold the UI components and integrate them into the main dashboard view.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Connect your repository to a CI/CD pipeline so every commit is a production-ready step.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a Vercel configuration file and a GitHub Actions workflow to automate testing and production deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure the CI/CD pipeline and verify the production build settings.'

pro_tips: Always build for multi-tenancy from Day 1; adding it later is a nightmare that kills project momentum.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests as you code; it prevents the 'I'll do it later' technical debt accumulation.
Treat your SaaS starter kit as a product itself—keep a 'master' repo that you fork for every new client project.
Focus on the 'Happy Path' for your MVP; ignore edge cases until you have your first paying user.
Keep your database schema outside the app code in a migration folder to ensure portability between different cloud providers.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, immediately set up a feedback loop by sharing it with three potential users. Use their friction points to iterate your next feature set, rather than guessing what to build.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop overthinking and start shipping. The bridge between 'no ideas' and 'shipped product' is just a well-executed starter kit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
